blocks.text: The create-session handler in apps/api/src/features/session/index.ts already calls env.RATE_LIMITER, but the binding is optional and unset in the current wrangler config, so every deployment today accepts unbounded writes. A single client looping on POST /api/session exhausts the D1 daily write quota in about four minutes at 200 requests per second.

blocks.title: This is reachable today
blocks.text: The endpoint is unauthenticated by design, so the abuse path needs no credentials and no prior session.

blocks.caption: Test coverage if we take the binding

items.label: Limiter absent in local dev
items.note: The handler skips the check; no test asserts the skip.

decisions.q: Which rate limiter should we put in front of POST /api/session?
decisions.why: The binding costs nothing to run and covers the abuse case we actually observed. The KV counter only pays off if we later need per-user quotas, which nothing in the current roadmap asks for.

opts.label: Cloudflare Rate Limiting binding
opts.detail: Ships in hours, no storage cost, but the key space is limited to what the binding exposes, so per-user quotas are out.

opts.label: Custom KV counter
opts.detail: Any key space we want, at one KV write per request and an eventual-consistency window that lets a burst through.

decisions.q: How should the limit reach production?
decisions.why: Shadow mode first is cheap insurance; the other two are independent and can be taken in any combination.

opts.label: Shadow mode for one week
opts.detail: Log what would have been blocked, block nothing.

opts.label: Alert on sustained 429s
opts.detail: A real client hitting the limit is a signal we set it too low.

opts.label: Raise the limit to 30 per minute
opts.detail: 10 per minute may be tight for an agent publishing several documents in one run.
